channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Casus Belli Guide—Where to Find Pillagers, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Casus Belli is the new Escape from Tarkov event, and it’s only set to run for a few days, so you’ll need to work fast if you want to soak up all the rewards. It’s available to all PvE and PvP players from April 9, and it starts with an easy task that’s also named Casus Belli.

If you want to learn how to solve Casus Belli as quickly as possible, find Pillagers in Escape from Tarkov, and find out what comes next, simply read on. We’ve got the full guide for this task, as easy as it is.

How to Find Pillagers in Escape from Tarkov

The core of Casus Belli revolves around finding and killing five Pillagers, a new faction added to Escape from Tarkov specifically for this event. You’ll notice that they’re wearing fancy bulletproof masks, which you’ll also need to collect to complete this task.

The task will be handed to you by Ref, so you’ll need to have him unlocked. Also, you’ll need to have wiped your account or made your account after the November 15 release of Escape from Tarkov 1.0.

Here’s the task description:

Heya, warrior! Got something for you, it’s urgent. I recently rolled out a new feature for the Arena, called it Seasons. Fighters aren’t just chasing cash anymore, they’re fighting for unique rewards. Whole system built around it… Turns out it’s a big hit. I’ve already got Season Three lined up, just waiting on a shipment of reward gear and some upgrade schematics. And now some piece of shit caught wind of the convoys and decided to mess with me.

Can you believe that? They’re trying to ruin a whole season the gladiators have been waiting on since New Year’s! Go take a look and find out who’s behind it. You should be able to spot them by their gear, Arena rewards stand out from standard kit after all. Find those bastards, deal with them, and bring me whatever you can pull off them. At least I’ll know how bad things are and which convoys they’ve already hit.

First, you need to eliminate five Pillagers, but where are they?

To find Pillagers, you’ll want to check the following locations:

Customs

  • Bridge
  • Stronghold

Woods

  • Rundown Village
  • Scav House / Checkpoint Road

Lighthouse

  • Long Road
  • Containers

Shoreline

  • Island
  • New Gas Station
  • Railway

Interchange

  • Scav Camp
  • Hydroelectric Power Station

Pillagers are tough enemies capable of overwhelming you pretty quickly, and they converge in groups of three to five combatants, so you’ll want to go equipped and loaded for a good fight. You’ll also find Pillagers guarding decent boxes of loot, so keep an eye open for those.

Don’t forget to take their masks, as you’ll need to hand three to Ref to complete this task.

Next, you’ll need to contend with Backup Plan, which we’ve also got a guide for.

Let us know if you’ve completed Casus Belli on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out everything you need to know about the new Escape from Tarkov event

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/tarkov-casus-belli-guide-where-pillagers/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Casus Belli Event is Locked Behind ‘Wipe Wall’
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has pushed live an all-new event in Escape from Tarkov dubbed ‘Casus Belli’, which is Latin for ’cause for war.’ It extends the previous events we’ve seen with smugglers popping up across Tarkov, but this time, Pillagers have come to roost in the Norvinsk region.

The event runs from today until April 15th, and fans of the pioneering extraction shooter are working now to uncover the string of missions associated with the event. As always, the introductory task, simply named Casus Belli, is the easiest and allows you to get to grips with the fundamentals of the event.

Latest Escape from Tarkov Event Adds New Enemies

When you venture into Escape from Tarkov today, you’ll notice that a new event has kicked off. However, to access the event, you’ll need to have either made your account or wiped your account after the 1.0 release, which landed on November 15, 2025.

If you’re playing on a PvE account and you’ve not wiped your profile (which wasn’t forced when the 1.0 release came around), you’ll find that you can’t access Casus Belli.

Admittedly, that should block the tiniest minority imaginable from playing the event, as almost everyone in the Escape from Tarkov ecosystem will have wiped several months ago. If you main PvP, you had no choice, and if you play PvE, you would have had to wipe to access the 1.0 content, including the new campaign.

It might be obvious, but the new Escape from Tarkov event is available to both PvE and PvP players, which means everyone can take advantage of the new entertainment on offer.

The first task is the one we know about right now, and it requires users to eliminate five Pillagers across Tarkov’s maps and return three found-in-raid painted CQCM masks, which are worn by the Pillagers.

We have a guide detailing how to complete this task and the follow-up ones.

Have you taken a deep dive into Casus Belli yet in Escape from Tarkov? Let us know what you’ve discovered on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the Call of Duty meta has been shaken up

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: SteamGPT is Reportedly in the Works for Support and CS2 Anti-Cheat
item.content:encoded:
According to new datamined information, Valve is seemingly working on SteamGPT to help with support and CS2 anti-cheat.

Steam just released a Workshop Beta update that adds quick-view previews, smarter filtering tools, and faster page updates to enhance the mod browsing experience. Now, it seems the devs at Valve are working on an AI-based tool that could help with other tasks across the platforms.

Dataminer Finds Mentions of SteamGPT

Reliable Steam dataminer Gabe Follower recently posted on X (formerly Twitter) about discovering “SteamGPT” in the code. This new AI-based tool is believed to assist with support on the platform and is reportedly connected to the Trust Score and anti-cheat measures for CS2. While Valve has not officially announced any updates regarding this feature, if it is indeed in development, it will likely be introduced in Steam Beta first.

Steam is consistently working to enhance the user experience for all players on its platform. Recently, the platform announced it was working on a feature that provides players with framerate data, based on information collected from users playing the game on similar configurations. However, with the datamined new AI-based tool, implementation will be the most important, as audience reception will depend on how well it manages support and other requests.

In other news, a recent claim states that  Valve is planning a 2028 release window for Steam Deck 2. Additionally, Counter-Strike: Global Offensive has found a new home on Steam, as Valve has reintroduced the game to the storefront. What are your thoughts on Valve bringing SteamGPT for support and CS2 Anti-Cheat? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Playground Productions’ announcement of the release of the Backyard Baseball demo. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Backyard Baseball Releases New Demo On Steam
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to enter the backyard. Backyard Sports has announced the release of its demo for the upcoming Backyard Baseball.

Available now on Steam for both PC and Mac, the demo features the game’s Backyard Derby mode.

Similar to the traditional Home Run Derby, Backyard Derby has you racing against the clock to hit as many home runs as possible. You’ll even get bonus points based on the distance of your home runs.

The demo features three players—Pablo Sanchez, Kenny Kawaguchi, and Kiesha Phillips—and you can play the mode either during the day or at night at the classic Steele Stadium. Players will be able to play solo or against friends, and there’s even a “crazy pitch” mode that has you trying to hit dingers while dealing with a variety of wacky pitches.

Insider Gaming recently sat down with Playground Productions founder and CEO Lindsay Barnett and developer Mega Cat Studios founder and CEO James Deighan to talk about Backyard Baseball and the franchise’s return. The full interview will be available later this month, but you can learn about how Barnett managed to secure the rights to Backyard Sports right now.

Backyard Baseball, the first game in the franchise in over a decade, is scheduled to release on July 9 for PC, PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch. The goal for Playground Productions and Mega Cat Studios is that this new game is the first in a new lineup of multi-sport titles.

Are you going to check out the Backyard Derby in the Backyard Baseball demo? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other gaming news, read about when you could expect to see the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake revealed. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Releases This Summer
item.content:encoded:
The release date for Rhythm Heaven Groove has been announced. The game will be released on July 2, 2026. The news was confirmed via the Nintendo Today app.

Rhythm Heaven Groove was first announced during a Nintendo Direct presentation on March 27, 2025. The game is one of the final titles announced prior to the release of the Nintendo Switch 2 and will also be available on the original Nintendo Switch.

Rhythm Heaven History

The game is the third in the franchise, which began with the original Rhythm Heaven, released on the Nintendo DS nearly 20 years ago, in July 2008. Rhythm Heaven Fever was released on the Nintendo Wii in February 2012, and now, more than a decade later, the series is back.

Alongside the release date, gameplay from one of the mini games in Rhythm Heaven Groove was shown. Slice N Dice Kitchen is a rhythm-based mini game where players catch vegetables and slice them to the beat.

More Potential Nintendo Announcements Incoming

While all players are waiting for the day that Nintendo holds its first general Nintendo Direct presentation of the year, there are some announcements rumored to be forthcoming. NateTheHate had previously revealed that a new Star Fox game is expected to be released this summer. Additionally, that game is expected to be revealed later this month.

Also, there is an expectation that a remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time will be forthcoming this year. Learn more about that at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide any and all updates concerning announcements from Nintendo.

What games are you hoping to see Nintendo release later this year? Are you excited for Rhythm Heaven Groove? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
